DataFlow Innovations

Staff Data Engineer

LOCATION

San Francisco, CA (Remote-
Friendly)

TYPE

Full-Time

COMPENSATION

$240,000 - $320,000 USD

TECH STACK

Apache Spark

Airflow

dbt

Snowflake

Python

SQL

Kafka

BigQuery

Data modeling (dimensional
modeling, data vault)

Terraform/Infrastructure-as-
Code

About DataFlow Innovations: DataFlow Innovations is a fast-
growing data infrastructure company founded in 2018,
specializing in building enterprise-grade data platforms and
analytics solutions for mid-market to Fortune 500 companies. We
help organizations ingest, transform, and operationalize massive

volumes of data across hybrid cloud environments. Our platform
serves over 200 customers across finance, healthcare, retail, and
technology sectors.

We're seeking an experienced Staff Data Engineer with 8+ years
of proven technical leadership to join our Data Platform team. In

this role, you'll architect and lead the design of large-scale data
pipelines, mentor a team of senior engineers, and establish
technical standards for data infrastructure across the
organization. You'll work on mission-critical data systems
supporting thousands of concurrent users while guiding
architectural decisions that impact company-wide data strategy.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Architect and design enterprise-scale data platforms
supporting petabyte-level data ingestion, transformation, and
analytics workloads using Apache Spark, Airflow, and cloud
data warehouses

Lead technical design reviews and provide architectural
guidance for complex data pipeline projects across multiple
teams, establishing best practices for data modeling, lineage
tracking, and quality assurance

Mentor and develop a team of 4-6 senior data engineers,
conducting code reviews, fostering technical growth, and
building a culture of excellence and knowledge-sharing

Establish and enforce data engineering standards, including
SQL best practices, dbt conventions, infrastructure-as-code
patterns, and CI/CD workflows for data products

Drive the adoption of new technologies and frameworks (e.g.,
Kafka for real-time streaming, advanced dbt patterns) by
conducting POCs, building reference implementations, and
training engineering teams

Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders including data
scientists, analytics engineers, and product teams to translate
business requirements into scalable technical solutions

Building the data infrastructure thatpowers real-time insights at scale Improve data platform reliability and performance by identifying
bottlenecks in Snowflake/BigQuery queries, optimizing Airflow
DAGs, and implementing monitoring and alerting strategies

Lead strategic initiatives for data platform modernization,
including cloud migration planning, disaster recovery design,
and cost optimization across data infrastructure

Contribute to open-source projects and present technical work
at internal tech talks and industry conferences

REQUIREMENTS

8+ years of hands-on data engineering experience with proven
track record designing and shipping production data systems
at scale

Expert-level proficiency in Python and SQL, with ability to write
performant queries and optimize complex transformations

5+ years of production experience with Apache Spark and
workflow orchestration tools (Airflow, Prefect, or Dagster), with
deep understanding of distributed computing concepts

3+ years of experience building and optimizing cloud data
warehouse solutions (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) including

schema design, partitioning strategies, and cost optimization

Demonstrated technical leadership: mentoring engineers,
driving architectural decisions, and influencing technical
direction across teams

Strong systems thinking and experience building data lineage,
quality frameworks, and metadata management systems

BONUS POINTS

Experience with dbt (data build tool) and modern ELT/dbt best
practices for data transformation at scale

Real-time data pipeline experience using Kafka, Spark
Streaming, or similar event streaming platforms

Hands-on experience with Iceberg, Delta Lake, or other open
table formats for data lakehouse architectures

Knowledge of data governance, data quality frameworks
(Great Expectations, dbt tests), and regulatory compliance
(GDPR, HIPAA)

Track record of successful hiring, team building, and
organizational scaling

BENEFITS

 Competitive salary and equity package with annual refreshing
grants

Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental, vision) with

90% employer premium coverage

401(k) match up to 6% with immediate vesting

Unlimited PTO with minimum 15 days encouraged per year

Remote-first work environment with flexible office access in SF,
NYC, and Austin

Annual professional development budget ($5,000) for
conferences, certifications, and courses

Parental leave (16 weeks primary caregiver, 12 weeks
secondary)

APPLY

Please submit your resume, a portfolio or GitHub profile
showcasing your data engineering work, and a brief cover letter
explaining your interest in data platform architecture to

careers@dataflow-innovations.com. In your application, highlight
your experience with large-scale data systems and any
leadership roles you've held. We review applications on a rolling
basis and aim to respond within 1 week.
